customers.contradiction: Mara wants perfect herbs for the cafe menu but hates admitting the alley is mostly shade.
customers.market_hint: If my parsley pot survives another week by the mop sink, I can sell soup without pretending garnish is optional.

customers.contradiction: Tovan teaches patience beautifully, then panic-buys anything that promises fast results.
customers.market_hint: The third-years are naming every seed tray. Please sell me something that will not break their hearts by Monday.

customers.contradiction: Cilla says she wants simple plants, but she keeps reaching for the fanciest tag.
customers.market_hint: The north side of my porch grows moss on a tin bucket. Is that a plant opinion or a personal insult?
